video:video.video:title: Taylor Russell says playing an alien is a gift
video:video.video:description: Taylor Russell explains why she jumped at the opportunity to play an alien in Korean movie "Hope" by Na Hong-jin.

video:video.video:title: France’s parliament votes to repeal slavery-era Black Code
video:video.video:description: For nearly two centuries after France abolished slavery, the colonial-era law that classified humans as property has remained quietly on its books.

video:video.video:title: Trump administration review may close some of Mexico's 53 US consulates
video:video.video:description: The Trump administration is conducting a review of the 53 Mexican consulates in the United States. The move could lead some of them to be closed.

video:video.video:title: Mendes intimidated by scale of 'Masters of the Universe'
video:video.video:description: What surprised Camila Mendes the most when she arrived to film live action movie, "Masters of the Universe"?

video:video.video:title: Chilean American stolen as a baby reunites with his mom and gets a second chance at family
video:video.video:description: A Denver man who was adopted by an American family and later learns he was stolen by his caretaker in Chile during the Pinochet years, is reunited with his birth mother after years of searching.

video:video.video:title: A first Ebola recovery in Ituri, but no specific treatment used
video:video.video:description: Dr Richard Kitengé said that they provided therapeutic medical care to the first Ebola patient that recovered, taking into account the patient’s clinical progress.

video:video.video:title: Brazil is set to join other Latin American countries with a 40-hour, 5-day workweek
video:video.video:description: Brazil was set to join other Latin American countries that have shortened working hours as the lower house approved a constitutional amendment on Wednesday establishing a 40-hour, five-day workweek.

video:video.video:title: UK's largest chalk hill figure, the Cerne Abbas Giant, gets a makeover
video:video.video:description: At 180 feet high, the giant is etched on the hillside above Cerne Abbas, a village in Dorset, England.

video:video.video:title: Under President Milei's austerity, disabled Argentines risk losing essential services
video:video.video:description: In recent months, the Argentine government has frozen payments to organizations providing therapeutic and educational services to people with disabilities.

video:video.video:title: Justice Department opens investigation into E. Jean Carroll, who accused Trump of assault: AP source
video:video.video:description: The Justice Department has opened an investigation into whether E. Jean Carroll lied during the course of civil litigation against President Donald Trump, according to a person familiar with the matter.

video:video.video:title: Brendan Fraser says starring in 'Pressure' taught him more about D-Day
video:video.video:description: Brendan Fraser got a humbling history lesson while starring in the World War II drama "Pressure." He plays Gen. Dwight D. Eisenhower in the film.

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrikes hit Lebanon ahead of crucial talks in Washington
video:video.video:description: Tensions have been increasing in southern Lebanon, where Israeli troops in recent days have crossed the strategic Litani River, which the Israeli military has used as a de facto boundary.

video:video.video:title: Middle East still on edge ahead of talks in Washington
video:video.video:description: U.S. and Iranian negotiators have reached a tentative agreement to extend the ceasefire by 60 days and launch talks on Iran’s nuclear program, according to a U.S. official familiar with the matter.

video:video.video:title: Conan O’Brien speaks at Harvard commencement as Trump tightens pressure on the school
video:video.video:description: Harvard graduates heard from television host Conan O’Brien at their commencement. The appearance of O'Brien, a Harvard alum, comes as the Ivy League school faces mounting pressure from President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: UK's mystery chalk hill figure, the Cerne Abbas Giant, gets a makeover
video:video.video:description: The outline of the UK's largest chalk hill figure, the Cerne Abbas Giant, is getting a revamp with 17 tonnes of wet chalk.

video:video.video:title: The Hoover Dam gets new look to mark America 250
video:video.video:description: The Hoover Dam is marking the Semiquincentennial for the United States with a 300 foot tall flag and lighting to match.

video:video.video:title: Otter pups begin swimming lessons at UK zoo
video:video.video:description: Three rare giant otter pups have begun swimming lessons at the UK's Chester Zoo. Led by mum Bonita and dad Manu, the rare pups were picked up by the scruff of their necks and popped straight in at the ‘deep end’.

video:video.video:title: En route to Sweden, Zelenskyy posts video of jets Ukraine expects to receive
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy published a video on Thursday that appeared to show him looking out of his plane window at the fighter jets his country hopes to receive.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy arrives in Sweden as Ukraine touts drone expertise
video:video.video:description: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy arrived in Sweden on Thursday for talks with Prime Minister Ulf Kristersson on bilateral defense cooperation, the Ukrainian leader and the Swedish government said.

video:video.video:title: Israeli drone strikes an apartment building in Sidon port city in Lebanon
video:video.video:description: The intensification of attacks comes after Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced an expansion in the Israeli military's attacks in Lebanon, apparently sparked by Hezbollah's use of fiber-optic exploding drones that have struck Israeli troops in Lebanon and reached some of Israel's northern bor

video:video.video:title: Galitzine has the power of the He-Man hair
video:video.video:description: Nicholas Galitzine, who plays He-Man in the new "Masters of the Universe" movie, explains why he has "heroic" hair.

video:video.video:title: Andrew Scott takes on the 'gripping story' of the meteorologist who saved D-Day
video:video.video:description: D-Day was supposed to happen on June 5, 1944, but ultimately took place the next day because of a Scottish meteorologist played by Andrew Scott in new drama "Pressure."

video:video.video:title: Muslims in India and Bangladesh join Eid al-Adha prayers
video:video.video:description: In India people gathered in the Jama Masjid Mosque, while worshippers in Bangladesh gathered at the capital's National Eidgah.

video:video.video:title: Muslim pilgrims continue stoning rituals in Mina for the second day
video:video.video:description: Pilgrims moved through the sprawling Jamarat complex in Saudi Arabia’s capital and threw pebbles at a pillar in a ritual seen as a symbolic stoning of the devil.

video:video.video:title: More than a dozen students killed in an overnight fire at a girls’ school in Kenya
video:video.video:description: At least 16 students died in an overnight fire that started in the dormitories of a girls’ boarding school in Kenya, a government official said, in the latest such incident to rock the East African nation.

video:video.video:title: The Hoover Dam marks America 250
video:video.video:description: The Hoover Dam is marking the United States’ 250th anniversary with a 300 foot tall flag and a lighting display.

video:video.video:title: A large fire has erupted at an apartment complex in Dallas
video:video.video:description: Firefighters are battling a large fire at an apartment complex in Dallas. The blaze erupted Thursday afternoon where debris from one collapsed building lay heaped on the ground while flames and black smoke billowed into the sky.

video:video.video:title: Candidates for California governor tangle in crowded race ahead of state primary, AP explains
video:video.video:description: Over 50 candidates are vying to replace Democratic Gov. Gavin Newsom, who is barred by law from seeking a third term, to lead the nation’s most populous state and one of the world’s largest economies.

video:video.video:title: Matthew Perry's parents arrive for sentencing of assistant over his death
video:video.video:description: Matthew Perry’s live-in personal assistant, who had a central role in the “Friends” actor’s descent into ketamine addiction and injected him with a fatal dose of the drug, was sentenced Wednesday to three years and five months in prison, bringing an end to the legal saga surrounding the death of one

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 28
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday May 28h: Multiple attacks reported in Persian Gulf region; Israeli strikes in Lebanon; Search continues for 9 missing in Washington state paper mill; U.S.

video:video.video:title: Students killed in an overnight fire at a girls' school
video:video.video:description: At least 16 students died and dozens were injured in an overnight fire that started in the dormitories of a girls’ boarding school in Kenya, a government official said.

video:video.video:title: New He-Man Nicholas Galitzine has the power
video:video.video:description: "Masters of the Universe" star Nicholas Galitzine steps into He-Man's boots for Trevor Knight's reboot, featuring The Darkness and Brian May on its soundtrack. (May 28)

video:video.video:title: Vance seems to move goal posts for achieving one of Trump’s key objectives for launching the war
video:video.video:description: The Trump administration said from the start of the war with Iran that they undertook it to ensure that the country could never have a nuclear weapon.

video:video.video:title: Divers find 5 villagers alive in a flooded cave in Laos after more than a week trapped in darkness
video:video.video:description: Five villagers who became trapped in a flooded cave in central Laos more than a week ago have been found alive by divers who discovered them sitting on a rock in the darkness, rescuers said Wednesday, but two others are still missing.

video:video.video:title: Aid supplies reach heart of Congo's Ebola outbreak as WHO head travels to Kinshasa
video:video.video:description: Aid workers rushed supplies Thursday to the center of Congo's outbreak of a rare type of Ebola virus while beleaguered medical personnel struggled with a lack of equipment, a distrustful population and armed groups in a volatile region.

video:video.video:title: Rocket belonging to Jeff Bezos’ Blue Origin explodes during test at launch pad
video:video.video:description: Rocket belonging to Jeff Bezos’ Blue Origin explodes during test at launch pad

video:video.video:title: Scientists says pigeons could have a 'gut feeling' in their liver to help them navigate
video:video.video:description: Scientists say they are closer to unravelling the mystery about how pigeons manage to find their way home, no matter how far away they are taken from it.

video:video.video:title: Guatemala's president denies report of US deal on anti-drug trafficking strikes
video:video.video:description: Guatemalan President Bernardo Arévalo on Thursday denied the existence of an agreement with the United States to conduct anti-drug trafficking operations on Guatemalan soil.

video:video.video:title: Sinner: I 'hit the wall' in shocking French Open exit
video:video.video:description: No. 1 Jannik Sinner said he “hit the wall” after a shocking second-round exit at the French Open, despite entering the tournament as the overwhelming favorite to win the clay-court Grand Slam.

video:video.video:title: Scott Bessent says steps have been taken to put Trump on a $250 bill
video:video.video:description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Thursday he doesn't think "there's anything untoward" about President Donald Trump's face appearing on the $250 bill in honor of the 250th anniversary of the United States, but he said the final decision is up to Congress.

video:video.video:title: Jill Biden feared Joe Biden was having a stroke during 2024 debate
video:video.video:description: Joe Biden's shaky, mumbling and sometimes confused delivery against Donald Trump in June 2024 gave fuel to questions voters already had about his fitness for a second term.

video:video.video:title: Mourners gather for the funeral of 10 people killed in Israeli strikes in Gaza
video:video.video:description: Dozens of Palestinians in Gaza City gathered on Thursday for funeral prayers for 10 people killed in overnight Israeli strikes, including five children and an elderly person.

video:video.video:title: Police say a man stabbed and wounded 3 people at a Swiss train station before being arrested
video:video.video:description: A man stabbed and wounded three people at the train station in the Swiss city of Winterthur on Thursday before being arrested, police said.

video:video.video:title: Water shortages worsen across Cuba as oil supplies dwindle
video:video.video:description: Nearly 3 million Cubans experience water shortages every day because of a severe oil shortage that government officials blame on a U.S. energy blockade.

video:video.video:title: French luxury brands celebrate 250 years of Franco-American relationship with new exhibit
video:video.video:description: A new exhibit titled “Hidden Treasures” shines a light on the 250 year cultural dialogue between France and the U.S. through the lens of French luxury goods.

video:video.video:title: FIFA faces investigation over soaring World Cup ticket prices
video:video.video:description: New York and New Jersey officials are investigating whether FIFA’s World Cup ticketing practices violated consumer protection laws after fans complained about soaring prices, variable pricing models and seat relocations.

video:video.video:title: France’s parliament votes to repeal slavery-era Black Code
video:video.video:description: The National Assembly voted 254-0 — a rare show of unanimity — to adopt a bill repealing the Code Noir, or Black Code, the 1685 decree King Louis XIV signed to govern slaves across France’s colonies.

video:video.video:title: Thalia reflects on friendship with Michael Jackson
video:video.video:description: While promoting her recent album, "Todo Suena Mejor en Cumbia," Thalia said her friendship with Michael Jackson was "beautiful" and says the two stars bonded over their shared experiences growing up in the global spotlight.

video:video.video:title: Colombians will vote in a high-stakes test of Gustavo Petro’s agenda
video:video.video:description: Colombia will elect a new president and vice president on May 31 in a vote that has been cast as a referendum on outgoing President Gustavo Petro’s policies.

video:video.video:title: 'Show me my daughter' cries a parent in anguish following deadly school fire
video:video.video:description: 'Show me my daughter!' a parent cried in anguish on Thursday following a deadly school fire in central Kenya, which killed at least 16 students, according to government officials.

video:video.video:title: Man wanted over 3 killings apprehended after an intense search of Hawaii’s Big Island
video:video.video:description: A man wanted in connection with the killings of three men has been apprehended after a massive search of Hawaii’s Big Island.

video:video.video:title: Is Woody Tom Hanks' most iconic character?
video:video.video:description: Tom Hanks says the success of Woody from the Toy Story franchise "blows me out of my hut."

video:video.video:title: Three people injured in Russian strikes on Ukraine's Zaporizhzhia region
video:video.video:description: Three people were injured in a Russian strike on the south eastern Ukraine city of Zaporizhzhia on Friday, the State Emergency Service said.

video:video.video:title: Israeli fighter jets strike southern Lebanese village after warning its residents to leave
video:video.video:description: Israel's fighter jets struck a southern Lebanese village Friday shortly after warning its residents to leave the area.

video:video.video:title: Israeli fighter jets strike southern Lebanese village after Israel warned residents to leave
video:video.video:description: Israel's fighter jets struck a southern Lebanese village Friday shortly after Israel warned the residents to leave the area.

video:video.video:title: Russian drone launched against Ukraine crashes in Romania, injuring 2
video:video.video:description: The drone was tracked by radar in Romanian airspace and crashed onto the roof of a building in Galati, Romania’s Defense Ministry said in a statement.

video:video.video:title: US-based Echodyne and Germany-based Argus Interception demonstrate their latest drone tech
video:video.video:description: In Wümme, Germany, on Thursday, U.S.-based Echodyne and Germany-based Argus Interception demonstrated their latest tech for the media.

video:video.video:title: Manhattan DA condemns man's 'shocking, horrible' acts in killing 4 sleeping homeless men
video:video.video:description: There was no one in the courtroom on Thursday to speak on behalf of the four men Randy Santos bludgeoned to death with a metal bar as they slept on the New York City streets.

video:video.video:title: What is Manhattanhenge? AP explains the New York phenomena
video:video.video:description: The setting sun was framed by a canyon of skyscrapers Thursday as it sunk below the horizon, perfectly aligned with the Manhattan street grid.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers evacuate the first of 5 villagers found trapped in a cave in Laos; 2 still missing
video:video.video:description: Rescue divers in Laos on Friday night safely evacuated the first of five local villagers who had been trapped in a cave for more than a week by floodwaters.

video:video.video:title: Nabatiyeh has been hit by near-daily airstrikes over the past two months
video:video.video:description: Buildings were damaged and rubble was spotted across Nabatiyeh on Friday following near-daily airstrikes in the Lebanese city over the past two months.

video:video.video:title: Funeral procession in southern Lebanon for Lebanese soldier killed in Israeli airstrike
video:video.video:description: Alaa Medlej was killed in an airstrike on a road near the village of Deir al-Zahrani, the army said.

video:video.video:title: Sue Tilley recounts her life-changing modeling for Lucian Freud. The result could fetch $47 million
video:video.video:description: Sue Tilley was working in an unemployment office when she met artist Lucian Freud. (AP Video by Kwiyeon Ha)

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 29
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday, May 29th: Tentative U.S.-Iran agreement reported; House; Members to question former Attorney Gen.

video:video.video:title: Singapore prepares for annual security forum
video:video.video:description: The Shangri-La Dialogue, hosted by the International Institute for Strategic Studies, also comes as the Middle East is increasingly on edge as new attacks have threatened the tenuous ceasefire in the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Could your next job interview be with an AI bot?
video:video.video:description: Imagine taking part in a job interview not conducted by a human, but an AI avatar... What might seem dystopian in concept is an increasing reality for some applicants.

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrike hits Gaza's Shati refugee camp after residents receive warning
video:video.video:description: An Israeli strike hit Gaza City's Shati refugee camp on Thursday night, hitting a building with no injuries reported.

video:video.video:title: Shrey Parikh dominates spell-off to win the National Spelling Bee
video:video.video:description: Shrey Parikh has won the Scripps National Spelling Bee, beating Ishaan Gupta in a lightning-round tiebreaker. Shrey is a 14-year-old from Rancho Cucamonga, California.

video:video.video:title: Mayor Mamdani commits to safety and protecting right to protest at NYC Israel Day parade
video:video.video:description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani confirmed he will not attend this year's Israel Day parade but said he is committed to ensuring the event is safe and secure.

video:video.video:title: AP photographer recalls ‘top secret’ trip to Baghdad with George W. Bush in 2003
video:video.video:description: AP Photographer Pablo Martínez Monsiváis shares a behind-the-scenes story of his “top secret” trip to Baghdad, Iraq, with President George W.

video:video.video:title: AP explains Blue Origin rocket explosion
video:video.video:description: Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin is assessing damage to its Florida launch pad after a rocket exploded during a test firing.

video:video.video:title: Pam Bondi faces House questioning over Epstein files release
video:video.video:description: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi is testifying before House lawmakers investigating Jeffrey Epstein's sexual abuse cases, a long-awaited appearance that brings fresh scrutiny of the Trump administration's botched release of the Epstein case files.

video:video.video:title: U.S. tomato prices are up 40%, the latest symbol of America’s affordability squeeze
video:video.video:description: Tomatoes, ubiquitous in everything from fast-food burgers to haute cuisine, are taking on a new role beyond the plate: A nagging reminder of rising costs.

video:video.video:title: Israeli troops enter southern Lebanese village under intense shelling
video:video.video:description: Israeli troops entered a southern Lebanese village early Friday under intense shelling, pushing deeper into the country as Lebanese and Israeli military officials began direct talks at the Pentagon over the deadly conflict.

video:video.video:title: Survivors of Epstein's abuse demand accountability as Bondi testifies before lawmakers
video:video.video:description: Former U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi appeared before House lawmakers as they investigate how the government has handled the investigations into Jeffrey Epstein.

video:video.video:title: Kenyan police arrest 8 students on suspicion of arson after deadly girls school fire
video:video.video:description: The girls were arrested for planning and carrying out a suspected arson attack at Utumishi Girls School in central Kenya, according to the Directorate of Criminal Investigations, or DCI, a department of the national police.

video:video.video:title: Humanoid robots and manual dexterity showcase in Japan humanoids summit
video:video.video:description: Mechanical hands claimed to be dexterous enough to thread a needle, childlike dancing robots and adult-sized ones to help with deliveries - they're all being presented as a future part of the workforce at Tokyo's Humanoids Summit.

video:video.video:title: Inauguration ceremony for new Patriarch of Chaldean Catholic Church in Iraq
video:video.video:description: Patriarch Mar Polis III Nona - born Amel Shamoon Nona - previously served as the Archbishop of Mosul and the Archbishop of Sydney before becoming the patriarch for the Chaldean Catholic Church.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers in Laos evacuate first of 5 villagers trapped in cave for more than a week
video:video.video:description: Rescue divers in Laos on Friday night safely evacuated the first of five local villagers who had been trapped in a cave for more than a week by floodwaters.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 29
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, May 29th: Pam Bondi refused to speak about Trump in meeting with lawmakers; A judge temporarily blocks ‘anti-weaponization’ settlement fund; 11 Lebanese killed by Israeli airstrikes; First of five trapped in Loas cave is rescued.

video:video.video:title: What to know about the New Jersey ICE facility protests and hunger strike
video:video.video:description: Protesters have clashed with armed federal immigration officers in front of a New Jersey detention center where advocates have asserted detainees are staging a hunger strike.

video:video.video:title: A decade after FARC peace deal, Colombia election tests competing paths to peace
video:video.video:description: Colombia is voting in its first round of presidential elections over the weekend in a race that has candidates grappling with the larger question of how to consolidate peace.

video:video.video:title: Bondi refuses to answer lawmakers' questions about Trump ties to release of Epstein files
video:video.video:description: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi refused to answer questions on President Donald Trump's ties to the release of the Epstein files as she appeared in a contentious meeting with a House lawmakers.

video:video.video:title: Pam Bondi arrives for House questioning over Epstein files release
video:video.video:description: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi is testifying before House lawmakers investigating Jeffrey Epstein's sexual abuse cases, a long-awaited appearance that brings fresh scrutiny of the Trump administration's botched release of the Epstein case files.

video:video.video:title: Pam Bondi arrives for House questioning over Epstein files release
video:video.video:description: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi is testifying before House lawmakers investigating Jeffrey Epstein's sexual abuse cases, a long-awaited appearance that brings fresh scrutiny of the Trump administration's botched release of the Epstein case files.

video:video.video:title: Judge says Kennedy Center board broke the law putting Trump's name on building
video:video.video:description: A federal judge ruled Friday that President Donald Trump’s name was added to the Kennedy Center illegally, and blocked the administration from closing the national cultural and arts center for major renovations.

video:video.video:title: Brendan Fraser says starring in 'Pressure' taught him more about D-Day
video:video.video:description: Brendan Fraser got a humbling history lesson while starring in the World War II drama "Pressure." He plays Gen. Dwight D. Eisenhower in the film.

video:video.video:title: Families speak after Canadian man pleads guilty to selling substances to people who killed themselves
video:video.video:description: A Canadian man accused of running an online operation selling lethal products in more than 40 countries has pleaded guilty to 14 counts of counseling or aiding suicide.

video:video.video:title: Mexico's Sheinbaum hands World Cup tickets to four young Mexican women ahead of tournament
video:video.video:description: Four young Mexican women who won tickets to the 2026 FIFA World Cup met President Claudia Sheinbaum on Friday after a nationwide contest aimed at promoting women’s soccer and giving fans a chance to attend matches in Mexico.

video:video.video:title: Migrant amateur teams in Greece get into World Cup spirit before new EU border measures take effect
video:video.video:description: Migrant and refugee teams in Greece's capital took part in a grassroots soccer tournament before the World Cup in North America.

video:video.video:title: Israeli soldiers share rare accounts from Gaza, describing ongoing killings despite ceasefire
video:video.video:description: The Israeli combat soldier saw his teammates yelling in celebration, congratulating one another. They had just struck a vehicle of Palestinians driving near the Israeli-controlled part of the Gaza Strip, killing everyone inside.
